name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: Cash America Pawn at 5901 Denton Hwy, Haltom City, TX 76148 is Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 3.3 stars across 71 reviews. Call +1 817-281-9054.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store chain with over 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is included in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating its substantial size and public market presence in the consumer finance sector.

FirstCash serves cash and credit-constrained consumers primarily through pawn loans secured by pledged personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and other merchandise). The company buys and sells merchandise across diverse categories, offers gold and precious metal purchasing, provides layaway services with 10% down payment options, and through its wholly-owned subsidiary AFF, provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through over 15,000 merchant partner locations nationwide.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its scale as the dominant international pawn operator with extensive geographic reach across North and Latin America, integrated retail merchandise operations alongside lending, and diversified revenue streams including gold buying and technology-driven payment solutions through AFF. Their non-recourse pawn loan structure means customers pledge collateral but are not personally liable for the loan amount—only the pledged item is at risk of forfeiture.

FirstCash is best suited for individuals needing immediate cash against personal property without credit checks, those seeking to buy quality merchandise at discounted prices, and consumers in areas with limited traditional credit access. However, pawn loans involve high effective interest rates despite being non-recourse, and customers risk losing pledged personal property if loans aren't repaid. The company's massive scale and public company status provide stability but don't change the fundamental economics of pawn lending.
pros: Non-recourse pawn loans—borrowers only risk the pledged item, not personal liability for loan amount
3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states plus UK and Latin America provide extensive geographic accessibility
No credit check required; immediate cash available regardless of credit history or debt
Diverse merchandise inventory (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments) allows customers to buy discounted items
Gold and precious metal buying service provides cash on the spot for those with valuables
Layaway service with only 10% down payment makes merchandise more accessible
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) provides corporate stability and transparency
cons: Pawn loans carry high effective interest rates due to short terms and fees, making them an expensive form of credit
Risk of losing pledged personal property if loan cannot be repaid by maturity date
Limited to consumers with valuable collateral to pledge; not accessible to those without eligible items
Retail merchandise prices, while discounted, depend on available inventory and may not offer significant savings
Layaway service ties up money for extended periods and may include additional fees not detailed on website
best_for: Individuals needing immediate emergency cash who have valuable personal property and limited credit options
Consumers seeking to purchase discounted merchandise (electronics, jewelry, tools, instruments) at below-retail prices
People with gold, silver, or platinum they wish to convert to cash quickly without selling through traditional channels
Cash-constrained borrowers who cannot qualify for traditional loans and need collateral-based lending solutions
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing immediate cash needs who have valuable personal property to pledge and lack access to traditional credit. The main caveat is that pawn loans are expensive short-term credit with the significant risk of losing pledged items if repayment deadlines are missed—they should be considered only after exploring payday alternatives, credit union personal loans, or employer advances.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Gold, silver, and precious metal buying and selling
Retail sales of new and used merchandise across multiple categories
Layaway services with 10% down payment option
Cash advances on pledged personal property with non-recourse terms
Lease-to-own payment solutions through subsidiary AFF (15,000+ merchant partners)
Retail finance payment solutions through AFF network
Store locator service to find nearest location
Online inventory browsing and merchandise search
Merchandise appraisal and valuation for pawn loans
Item purchase services (buying valuables for immediate cash)
Consumer education through Pawn FAQs and How To guides
